original_hypothesis.title: AgentVeToken.setCanStake Founder Toggle Enables Governance Capture via Staking Lock-Out
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The founder can call setCanStake(false) at any time to disable public staking. After accumulating governance power via initial staking, the founder toggles canStake to false, preventing new stakers from diluting their voting power. The founder (or their delegatees) then maintain permanent governance majority without risk of being outvoted. They can toggle canStake back temporarily to allow selected allies to stake.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The canStake boolean is a simple toggle with no cooldown, timelock, or governance vote required. Combined with the non-transferable nature of veTokens, this gives the founder unilateral control over who can participate in governance.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_msgSender() == founder) — only founder check, no timelock or governance requirement

preconditions: Agent was initially set with canStake=true (public)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: canStake toggle has zero cost and no delay
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Founder stakes, delegates to ally validators, then disables public staking. No new governance participants can join.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Founder maintains permanent governance majority, can pass any proposals including diverting agent revenue or changing metadata

economic_questions: What governance actions can the DAO take that have economic value?
Can the founder capture agent revenue via DAO proposals?
How much LP value can the founder control via governance monopoly?
validation_requirements: Map all DAO-executable actions and their economic impact
Verify no timelock or governance check on setCanStake
Test: stake → setCanStake(false) → verify no one else can stake

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Initial stake (already deposited as part of agent creation)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After initial governance power is established

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Would-be stakers and existing minority stakers

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current canStake state for deployed agents
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Map founder's governance power vs total supply

validation_result.mechanism: founder governance capture via staking lock-out
validation_result.impact_summary: The founder can unilaterally disable staking at any time, preventing new users from acquiring voting power and ensuring permanent governance control.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `AgentVeToken` contract includes a `setCanStake` function that allows the `founder` to toggle the `canStake` boolean at any time without any timelock, cooldown, or DAO approval. When `canStake` is set to `false`, the `stake` function reverts for any new stakers (since `totalSupply() > 0` after the initial LP staking during agent creation). Because `veToken`s are non-transferable, users cannot acquire voting power through any other means. This allows a malicious or compromised founder to wait until they have a comfortable governance majority, then call `setCanStake(false)` to permanently lock out any new participants, effectively capturing the DAO and centralizing control.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The `setCanStake` function in `AgentVeToken.sol` only checks `require(_msgSender() == founder, "Not founder");` and immediately updates `canStake`. The `stake` function checks `require(canStake || totalSupply() == 0, "Staking is disabled for private agent");`. Since the initial execution of the agent application stakes the initial LP tokens, `totalSupply()` will be greater than 0. Thus, setting `canStake` to false completely disables staking for everyone.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This function allows the founder to arbitrarily toggle the staking status at any time without DAO approval or a timelock.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
function stake(uint256 amount, address receiver, address delegatee) public {
        require(canStake || totalSupply() == 0, "Staking is disabled for private agent"); // Either public or first staker
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When `canStake` is false and the initial stake has already occurred (`totalSupply() > 0`), this require statement will revert any new staking attempts, locking out new users.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: 1. The founder calls `setCanStake(false)`. 2. `canStake` is updated to `false`. 3. A user attempts to call `stake(amount, receiver, delegatee)`. 4. The `stake` function evaluates `require(canStake || totalSupply() == 0)`. Since `canStake` is false and `totalSupply() > 0`, the call reverts. 5. The user is prevented from staking and acquiring voting power.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is highly realistic. A founder creates a public agent, waits for initial liquidity and participation, and then calls `setCanStake(false)`. From that point on, no new users can stake LP tokens to receive `veToken`s. Because `veToken`s are non-transferable, the existing distribution of voting power is permanently frozen. The founder, holding the initial stake, maintains a permanent governance majority and can unilaterally pass any DAO proposals.
validation_result.impact_assessment: This vulnerability completely undermines the decentralized governance model of the protocol. It allows the founder to rug-pull governance rights from the community, turning a supposedly decentralized DAO into a centralized entity controlled entirely by the founder.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the `setCanStake` function entirely if agents are meant to be permanently public or private upon creation.
If toggling is required, transfer the permission to call `setCanStake` to the DAO itself, requiring a governance vote to change the public/private status.
Implement a timelock or delay for changing the `canStake` status so users have time to react before staking is disabled.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy the protocol and create an agent via the factory. 2. Ensure the initial LP is staked, giving the founder `veToken`s. 3. As the founder, call `setCanStake(false)` on the `AgentVeToken` contract. 4. As a different user, acquire LP tokens and attempt to call `stake`. 5. Observe the transaction revert with 'Staking is disabled for private agent'.
